sections.title: Talking to Hudon while standing near the house

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Almera.

lines.speaker: Almera
lines.text: Hello adventurer, have you seen my boy yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid not, but I'm sure he hasn't gone far.

lines.speaker: Almera
lines.text: I do hope so, you can't be too careful these days.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Gerald
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you seen a small boy?

lines.speaker: Gerald
lines.text: Nope, plenty of small fish though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Almera
lines.text: Ah, hello there. Nice to see an outsider for a change. Are you busy? I have a problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I help?

lines.speaker: Almera
lines.text: It's my son Hudon, he's always getting into trouble. The boy's convinced there's hidden treasure in the river and I'm a bit worried about his safety, the poor lad can't even swim.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could go and take a look for you if you like?

lines.speaker: Almera
lines.text: Would you? You are kind. You can use the small raft out back if you wish, do be careful, the current down stream is very strong.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I'm in a rush.

lines.speaker: Almera
lines.text: Oh okay, never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello son, are you okay? You need help?

lines.speaker: Hudon
lines.text: It looks like you need the help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your mum sent me to find you.

lines.speaker: Hudon
lines.text: Don't play nice with me, I know you're looking for the treasure too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is this treasure you talk of?

lines.speaker: Hudon
lines.text: Just because I'm small doesn't mean I'm dumb! If I told you, you would take it all for yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I could help.

lines.speaker: Hudon
lines.text: I'm fine alone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... I wonder what this treasure is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're still here.

lines.speaker: Hudon
lines.text: I'll find that treasure soon, just you wait and see.

sections.title: Getting washed down the waterfall after talking to Hudon

lines.speaker: Gerald
lines.text: Blimey! Where did you come from? Not another one of those treasure hunters are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Treasure hunters?

lines.speaker: Gerald
lines.text: They say there's treasure hidden within the waterfall, left behind by the old elven king. Not that anyone's ever found anything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting, is there somewhere I can learn more about this?

lines.speaker: Gerald
lines.text: You could ask Hadley the tourist guide. He'll be in this building just here.

sections.title: Talking to Almera after talking to Hudon

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Almera
lines.text: Well hello, you're still around then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I saw Hudon by the river but he refused to come back with me.

lines.speaker: Almera
lines.text: Yes he told me, the foolish lad came in drenched to the bone, he had fallen into the waterfall, lucky he wasn't killed! Now he can spend the rest of the summer in his room.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any ideas what I could do while I'm here?

lines.speaker: Almera
lines.text: Why don't you visit the tourist centre south of the waterfall?

sections.title: Talking to Gerald again before learning about the treasure

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Gerald
lines.text: Hello traveller. Are you here to fish or to hunt for treasure?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you say that?

lines.speaker: Gerald
lines.text: Adventurers pass through here every week, they never find anything though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What treasure are they looking for?

lines.speaker: Gerald
lines.text: They say there's treasure hidden within the waterfall, left behind by the old elven king. Not that anyone's ever found anything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting, is there somewhere I can learn more about this?

lines.speaker: Gerald
lines.text: You could ask Hadley the tourist guide. He'll be in this building just here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Hadley
lines.text: Are you on holiday? If so you've come to the right place. I'm Hadley the tourist guide, anything you need to know just ask me. We have some of the most unspoilt wildlife and scenery in Gielinor.

lines.speaker: Hadley
lines.text: People come from miles around to fish in the clear lakes or to wander the beautiful hillsides.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It is quite pretty.

lines.speaker: Hadley
lines.text: Surely pretty is an understatement kind sir. Beautiful, amazing or possibly life-changing would be more suitable wording. Have you seen Baxtorian Falls? Named after the elven king who was buried beneath them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me what happened to the elven king?

lines.speaker: Hadley
lines.text: Baxtorian? I guess he died a long long time ago, it's quite sad really. After leaving his kingdom to deal with invading forces, Baxtorian returned to find his wife Glarial had been captured by the enemy.

lines.speaker: Hadley
lines.text: This destroyed Baxtorian, after years of searching he became a recluse. He went into the secret home he had made for Glarial under the waterfall and sealed himself in. To this day, no one has managed to enter.

lines.speaker: Hadley
lines.text: Anyway, I believe we have a book on him upstairs if you want to learn more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where else is worth visiting around here?

lines.speaker: Hadley
lines.text: There is a lovely spot for a picnic on the hill to the north east, there's a monument to the elven queen Glarial. It really is quite pretty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who was Glarial?

lines.speaker: Hadley
lines.text: Baxtorian's wife, the only other person who could also enter the waterfall apart from him. She was queen when this land was inhabited by elven kind.

lines.speaker: Hadley
lines.text: Glarial was kidnapped while Baxtorian was away, but they eventually recovered her body and brought her home to rest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's sad.

lines.speaker: Hadley
lines.text: True, I believe we have a book on Baxtorian and Glarial upstairs if you want to learn more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there treasure under the waterfall?

lines.speaker: Hadley
lines.text: Ha ha... Another treasure hunter. Well if there is no one's been able to get to it. They've been searching that river for decades, all to no avail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, goodbye.

lines.speaker: Hadley
lines.text: Enjoy your visit.

sections.title: Talking to Almera after reading the book

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again Almera.

lines.speaker: Almera
lines.text: Well hello again brave adventurer, are you enjoying the tranquil scenery of these parts?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, very relaxing.

lines.speaker: Almera
lines.text: Well I'm glad to hear it The authorities wanted to dig up this whole area for a mine, but the few locals who lived here wouldn't budge and they gave up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good for you.

lines.speaker: Almera
lines.text: Good for all of us!

sections.title: Talking to Hudon after reading the book

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Hudon.

lines.speaker: Hudon
lines.text: Oh it's you, trying to find my treasure again are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didn't know it belonged to you.

lines.speaker: Hudon
lines.text: It will do when I find it. I just need to get into this blasted waterfall I've been washed downstream three times already.

sections.title: Opening door to Golrie from the outside

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, are you okay?

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: It's just those blasted hob-gobs. I locked myself in here for protection. But I've left the key somewhere and now I'm stuck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... I'll have a look for a key.

sections.title: Opening door to Golrie from the inside
Searching the crate with the key

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Golrie.

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you mind if I have another look through this stuff?

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: No, of course not.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, who are you?

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: I'm Golrie, I've been stuck in here for ages. those hob-gobs are trying to steal my family's heirlooms. My grandad gave me all sorts of old junk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you mind if I have a look?

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: No, of course not.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I take this old pebble?

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: Oh that, yes have it, it's just some old elven junk I believe.

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: Thanks a lot for the key traveller. I think I'll wait in here until those hob-gobs get bored and leave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, take care Golrie.

sections.title: Using Glarial's pebble on Glarial's Tombstone
Talking to Almera after visiting Glarial's tomb

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Almera.

lines.speaker: Almera
lines.text: Hello adventurer, how's your treasure hunt going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I'm just sight seeing.

lines.speaker: Almera
lines.text: No adventurer stays here this long just to sight see. But your business is yours alone, if you need to use the raft go ahead. But please try not crash it this time!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Almera.

sections.title: Talking to Hudon after visiting Glarial's tomb

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Hudon
lines.text: Not you still, why don't you give up?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And miss all the fun!

lines.speaker: Hudon
lines.text: You do understand that anything you find you have to share with me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why's that?

lines.speaker: Hudon
lines.text: Because I told you about the treasure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I wouldn't count on it.

lines.speaker: Hudon
lines.text: That's not fair.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Neither is life kid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ouch!

sections.title: Using rope on the Dead Tree
Opening the door to the Waterfall dungeon

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ouch!

sections.title: Opening the door to Baxtorian's tomb
Searching the crate in the Waterfall dungeon
Using Glarial's amulet on the Statue of Glarial
Using Glarial's urn on the chalice
Talking to Hudon after the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Hudon
lines.text: You stole my treasure. I saw you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll make sure it goes to a good cause.

lines.speaker: Hudon
lines.text: Hmmmm!
